item.title: AnalogRetriever: Learning Cross-Modal Representations for Analog Circuit Retrieval
item.description:
By Yihan Wang, Lei Li, Yao Lai, Jing Wang, Yan Lu
Analog circuit design relies heavily on reusing existing intellectual property (IP), yet searching across heterogeneous representations such as SPICE netlists, schematics, and functional descriptions remains challenging. Existing methods are largely limited to exact matching within a single modality...

item.title: OceanPile: A Large-Scale Multimodal Ocean Corpus for Foundation Models
item.description:
By Yida Xue, Ningyu Zhang, Tingwei Wu, Zhe Ma, Daxiong Ji, et al.
The vast and underexplored ocean plays a critical role in regulating global climate and supporting marine biodiversity, yet artificial intelligence has so far delivered limited impact in this domain due to a fundamental data bottleneck. Specifically, ocean data are highly fragmented across disparate...

item.title: Talker-T2AV: Joint Talking Audio-Video Generation with Autoregressive Diffusion Modeling
item.description:
By Zhen Ye, Xu Tan, Aoxiong Yin, Hongzhan Lin, Guangyan Zhang, et al.
Joint audio-video generation models have shown that unified generation yields stronger cross-modal coherence than cascaded approaches. However, existing models couple modalities throughout denoising via pervasive attention, treating high-level semantics and low-level details in a fully entangled man...

item.title: Safety Drift After Fine-Tuning: Evidence from High-Stakes Domains
item.description:
By Emaan Bilal Khan, Amy Winecoff, Miranda Bogen, Dylan Hadfield-Menell
Foundation models are routinely fine-tuned for use in particular domains, yet safety assessments are typically conducted only on base models, implicitly assuming that safety properties persist through downstream adaptation. We test this assumption by analyzing the safety behavior of 100 models, incl...

item.title: Learning from Noisy Preferences: A Semi-Supervised Learning Approach to Direct Preference Optimization
item.description:
By Xinxin Liu, Ming Li, Zonglin Lyu, Yuzhang Shang, Chen Chen
Human visual preferences are inherently multi-dimensional, encompassing aesthetics, detail fidelity, and semantic alignment. However, existing datasets provide only single, holistic annotations, resulting in severe label noise: images that excel in some dimensions but are deficient in others are sim...

item.title: Nemotron 3 Nano Omni: Efficient and Open Multimodal Intelligence
item.description:
By NVIDIA, Amala Sanjay Deshmukh, Kateryna Chumachenko, Tuomas Rintamaki, Matthieu Le, et al.
We introduce Nemotron 3 Nano Omni, the latest model in the Nemotron multimodal series and the first to natively support audio inputs alongside text, images, and video. Nemotron 3 Nano Omni delivers consistent accuracy improvements over its predecessor, Nemotron Nano V2 VL, across all modalities, ena...

item.title: From Skill Text to Skill Structure: The Scheduling-Structural-Logical Representation for Agent Skills
item.description:
By Qiliang Liang, Hansi Wang, Zhong Liang, Yang Liu
LLM agents increasingly rely on reusable skills, capability packages that combine instructions, control flow, constraints, and tool calls. In most current agent systems, however, skills are still represented by text-heavy artifacts, including SKILL.md-style documents and structured records whose mac...

item.title: Soft Anisotropic Diagrams for Differentiable Image Representation
item.description:
By Laki Iinbor, Zhiyang Dou, Wojciech Matusik
We introduce Soft Anisotropic Diagrams (SAD), an explicit and differentiable image representation parameterized by a set of adaptive sites in the image plane. In SAD, each site specifies an anisotropic metric and an additively weighted distance score, and we compute pixel colors as a softmax blend o...

item.title: Co-Evolving Policy Distillation
item.description:
By Naibin Gu, Chenxu Yang, Qingyi Si, Chuanyu Qin, Dingyu Yao, et al.
RLVR and OPD have become standard paradigms for post-training. We provide a unified analysis of these two paradigms in consolidating multiple expert capabilities into a single model, identifying capability loss in different ways: mixed RLVR suffers from inter-capability divergence cost, while the pi...

item.title: Length Value Model: Scalable Value Pretraining for Token-Level Length Modeling
item.description:
By Zhen Zhang, Changyi Yang, Zijie Xia, Zhen Yang, Chengzhi Liu, et al.
Token serves as the fundamental unit of computation in modern autoregressive models, and generation length directly influences both inference cost and reasoning performance. Despite its importance, existing approaches lack fine-grained length modeling, operating primarily at the coarse-grained seque...

item.title: Efficient Training on Multiple Consumer GPUs with RoundPipe
item.description:
By Yibin Luo, Shiwei Gao, Huichuan Zheng, Youyou Lu, Jiwu Shu
Fine-tuning Large Language Models (LLMs) on consumer-grade GPUs is highly cost-effective, yet constrained by limited GPU memory and slow PCIe interconnects. Pipeline parallelism combined with CPU offloading mitigates these hardware bottlenecks by reducing communication overhead. However, existing PP...

item.title: Compliance versus Sensibility: On the Reasoning Controllability in Large Language Models
item.description:
By Xingwei Tan, Marco Valentino, Mahmud Elahi Akhter, Yuxiang Zhou, Maria Liakata, et al.
Large Language Models (LLMs) are known to acquire reasoning capabilities through shared inference patterns in pre-training data, which are further elicited via Chain-of-Thought (CoT) practices. However, whether fundamental reasoning patterns, such as induction, deduction, and abduction, can be decou...

item.title: ViPO: Visual Preference Optimization at Scale
item.description:
By Ming Li, Jie Wu, Justin Cui, Xiaojie Li, Rui Wang, et al.
While preference optimization is crucial for improving visual generative models, how to effectively scale this paradigm remains largely unexplored. Current open-source preference datasets contain conflicting preference patterns, where winners excel in some dimensions but underperform in others. Naiv...

item.title: Step-level Optimization for Efficient Computer-use Agents
item.description:
By Jinbiao Wei, Kangqi Ni, Yilun Zhao, Guo Gan, Arman Cohan
Computer-use agents provide a promising path toward general software automation because they can interact directly with arbitrary graphical user interfaces instead of relying on brittle, application-specific integrations. Despite recent advances in benchmark performance, strong computer-use agents r...

item.title: Web2BigTable: A Bi-Level Multi-Agent LLM System for Internet-Scale Information Search and Extraction
item.description:
By Yuxuan Huang, Yihang Chen, Zhiyuan He, Yuxiang Chen, Ka Yiu Lee, et al.
Agentic web search increasingly faces two distinct demands: deep reasoning over a single target, and structured aggregation across many entities and heterogeneous sources. Current systems struggle on both fronts. Breadth-oriented tasks demand schema-aligned outputs with wide coverage and cross-entit...

item.title: Prox-E: Fine-Grained 3D Shape Editing via Primitive-Based Abstractions
item.description:
By Etai Sella, Hao Phung, Nitay Amiel, Or Litany, Or Patashnik, et al.
Text-based 2D image editing models have recently reached an impressive level of maturity, motivating a growing body of work that heavily depends on these models to drive 3D edits. While effective for appearance-based modifications, such 2D-centric 3D editing pipelines often struggle with fine-graine...

item.title: Better Models, Faster Training: Sigmoid Attention for single-cell Foundation Models
item.description:
By Vijay Sadashivaiah, Georgios Dasoulas, Judith Mueller, Soumya Ghosh
Training stable biological foundation models requires rethinking attention mechanisms: we find that using sigmoid attention as a drop in replacement for softmax attention a) produces better learned representations: on six diverse single-cell datasets, sigmoid achieves 25% higher cell-type separation...

item.title: ComboStoc: Combinatorial Stochasticity for Diffusion Generative Models
item.description:
By Rui Xu, Jiepeng Wang, Hao Pan, Yang Liu, Xin Tong, et al.
In this paper, we study an under-explored but important factor of diffusion generative models, i.e., the combinatorial complexity. Data samples are generally high-dimensional, and for various structured generation tasks, additional attributes are combined to associate with data samples. We show that...

item.title: InteractWeb-Bench: Can Multimodal Agent Escape Blind Execution in Interactive Website Generation?
item.description:
By Qiyao Wang, Haoran Hu, Longze Chen, Hongbo Wang, Hamid Alinejad-Rokny, et al.
With the advancement of multimodal large language models (MLLMs) and coding agents, the website development has shifted from manual programming to agent-based project-level code synthesis. Existing benchmarks rely on idealized assumptions, especially for well-structured, information-rich inputs and ...

item.title: ExoActor: Exocentric Video Generation as Generalizable Interactive Humanoid Control
item.description:
By Yanghao Zhou, Jingyu Ma, Yibo Peng, Zhenguo Sun, Yu Bai, et al.
Humanoid control systems have made significant progress in recent years, yet modeling fluent interaction-rich behavior between a robot, its surrounding environment, and task-relevant objects remains a fundamental challenge. This difficulty arises from the need to jointly capture spatial context, tem...

item.title: Leveraging Verifier-Based Reinforcement Learning in Image Editing
item.description:
By Hanzhong Guo, Jie Wu, Jie Liu, Yu Gao, Zilyu Ye, et al.
While Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback (RLHF) has become a pivotal paradigm for text-to-image generation, its application to image editing remains largely unexplored. A key bottleneck is the lack of a robust general reward model for all editing tasks. Existing edit reward models usually gi...

item.title: Claw-Eval-Live: A Live Agent Benchmark for Evolving Real-World Workflows
item.description:
By Chenxin Li, Zhengyang Tang, Huangxin Lin, Yunlong Lin, Shijue Huang, et al.
LLM agents are expected to complete end-to-end units of work across software tools, business services, and local workspaces. Yet many agent benchmarks freeze a curated task set at release time and grade mainly the final response, making it difficult to evaluate agents against evolving workflow deman...

item.title: Instruction-Guided Poetry Generation in Arabic and Its Dialects
item.description:
By Abdelrahman Sadallah, Kareem Elozeiri, Mervat Abassy, Rania Elbadry, Mohamed Anwar, et al.
Poetry has long been a central art form for Arabic speakers, serving as a powerful medium of expression and cultural identity. While modern Arabic speakers continue to value poetry, existing research on Arabic poetry within Large Language Models (LLMs) has primarily focused on analysis tasks such as...

item.title: FlashRT: Towards Computationally and Memory Efficient Red-Teaming for Prompt Injection and Knowledge Corruption
item.description:
By Yanting Wang, Chenlong Yin, Ying Chen, Jinyuan Jia
Long-context large language models (LLMs)-for example, Gemini-3.1-Pro and Qwen-3.5-are widely used to empower many real-world applications, such as retrieval-augmented generation, autonomous agents, and AI assistants. However, security remains a major concern for their widespread deployment, with th...

item.title: When Do Diffusion Models learn to Generate Multiple Objects?
item.description:
By Yujin Jeong, Arnas Uselis, Iro Laina, Seong Joon Oh, Anna Rohrbach
Text-to-image diffusion models achieve impressive visual fidelity, yet they remain unreliable in multi-object generation. Despite extensive empirical evidence of these failures, the underlying causes remain unclear. We begin by asking how much of this limitation arises from the data itself. To disen...

item.title: MASCing: Configurable Mixture-of-Experts Behavior via Activation Steering Masks
item.description:
By Jona te Lintelo, Lichao Wu, Marina Krček, Sengim Karayalçin, Stjepan Picek
Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) architectures in Large Language Models (LLMs) have significantly reduced inference costs through sparse activation. However, this sparse activation paradigm also introduces new safety challenges. Since only a subset of experts is engaged for each input, model behavior become...

item.title: Repetition over Diversity: High-Signal Data Filtering for Sample-Efficient German Language Modeling
item.description:
By Ansar Aynetdinov, Patrick Haller, Alan Akbik
Recent research has shown that filtering massive English web corpora into high-quality subsets significantly improves training efficiency. However, for high-resource non-English languages like German, French, or Japanese, aggressive filtering creates a strategic dilemma: should practitioners priorit...

item.title: Let ViT Speak: Generative Language-Image Pre-training
item.description:
By Yan Fang, Mengcheng Lan, Zilong Huang, Weixian Lei, Yunqing Zhao, et al.
In this paper, we present Generative Language-Image Pre-training (GenLIP), a minimalist generative pretraining framework for Vision Transformers (ViTs) designed for multimodal large language models (MLLMs). To better align vision encoders with the autoregressive nature of LLMs, GenLIP trains a ViT t...

item.title: End-to-End Autoregressive Image Generation with 1D Semantic Tokenizer
item.description:
By Wenda Chu, Bingliang Zhang, Jiaqi Han, Yizhuo Li, Linjie Yang, et al.
Autoregressive image modeling relies on visual tokenizers to compress images into compact latent representations. We design an end-to-end training pipeline that jointly optimizes reconstruction and generation, enabling direct supervision from generation results to the tokenizer. This contrasts with ...

item.title: Map2World: Segment Map Conditioned Text to 3D World Generation
item.description:
By Jaeyoung Chung, Suyoung Lee, Jianfeng Xiang, Jiaolong Yang, Kyoung Mu Lee
3D world generation is essential for applications such as immersive content creation or autonomous driving simulation. Recent advances in 3D world generation have shown promising results; however, these methods are constrained by grid layouts and suffer from inconsistencies in object scale throughou...

item.title: UniVidX: A Unified Multimodal Framework for Versatile Video Generation via Diffusion Priors
item.description:
By Houyuan Chen, Hong Li, Xianghao Kong, Tianrui Zhu, Shaocong Xu, et al.
Recent progress has shown that video diffusion models (VDMs) can be repurposed for diverse multimodal graphics tasks. However, existing methods often train separate models for each problem setting, which fixes the input-output mapping and limits the modeling of correlations across modalities. We pre...

item.title: Themis: Training Robust Multilingual Code Reward Models for Flexible Multi-Criteria Scoring
item.description:
By Indraneil Paul, Glavaš Glavas, Iryna Gurevych
Reward models (RMs) have become an indispensable fixture of the language model (LM) post-training playbook, enabling policy alignment and test-time scaling. Research on the application of RMs in code generation, however, has been comparatively sparse, with existing work largely focusing on execution...

item.title: Learning while Deploying: Fleet-Scale Reinforcement Learning for Generalist Robot Policies
item.description:
By Yi Wang, Xinchen Li, Pengwei Xie, Pu Yang, Buqing Nie, et al.
Generalist robot policies increasingly benefit from large-scale pretraining, but offline data alone is insufficient for robust real-world deployment. Deployed robots encounter distribution shifts, long-tail failures, task variations, and human correction opportunities that fixed demonstration datase...

item.title: LASE: Language-Adversarial Speaker Encoding for Indic Cross-Script Identity Preservation
item.description:
By Venkata Pushpak Teja Menta
A speaker encoder used in multilingual voice cloning should treat the same speaker identically regardless of which script the audio was uttered in. Off-the-shelf encoders do not, and the failure is accent-conditional. On a 1043-pair Western-accented voice corpus across English, Hindi, Telugu, and Ta...

item.title: Learning to Act and Cooperate for Distributed Black-Box Consensus Optimization
item.description:
By Zi-Bo Qin, Feng-Feng Wei, Tai-You Chen, Wei-Neng Chen
Distributed blackbox consensus optimization is a fundamental problem in multi-agent systems, where agents must improve a global objective using only local objective queries and limited neighbor communication. Existing methods largely rely on handcrafted update rules and static cooperation patterns, ...

item.title: Online Self-Calibration Against Hallucination in Vision-Language Models
item.description:
By Minghui Chen, Chenxu Yang, Hengjie Zhu, Dayan Wu, Zheng Lin, et al.
Large Vision-Language Models (LVLMs) often suffer from hallucinations, generating descriptions that include visual details absent from the input image. Recent preference alignment methods typically rely on supervision distilled from stronger models such as GPT. However, this offline paradigm introdu...

item.title: Trees to Flows and Back: Unifying Decision Trees and Diffusion Models
item.description:
By Sai Niranjan Ramachandran, Suvrit Sra
Decision trees and diffusion models are ostensibly disparate model classes, one discrete and hierarchical, the other continuous and dynamic. This work unifies the two by establishing a crisp mathematical correspondence between hierarchical decision trees and diffusion processes in appropriate limiti...

item.title: Stable-GFlowNet: Toward Diverse and Robust LLM Red-Teaming via Contrastive Trajectory Balance
item.description:
By Minchan Kwon, Sunghyun Baek, Minseo Kim, Jaemyung Yu, Dongyoon Han, et al.
Large Language Model (LLM) Red-Teaming, which proactively identifies vulnerabilities of LLMs, is an essential process for ensuring safety. Finding effective and diverse attacks in red-teaming is important, but achieving both is challenging. Generative Flow Networks (GFNs) that perform distribution m...

item.title: Odysseus: Scaling VLMs to 100+ Turn Decision-Making in Games via Reinforcement Learning
item.description:
By Chengshuai Shi, Wenzhe Li, Xinran Liang, Yizhou Lu, Wenjia Yang, et al.
Given the rapidly growing capabilities of vision-language models (VLMs), extending them to interactive decision-making tasks such as video games has emerged as a promising frontier. However, existing approaches either rely on large-scale supervised fine-tuning (SFT) on human trajectories or apply re...

item.title: Code World Model Preparedness Report
item.description:
By Daniel Song, Peter Ney, Cristina Menghini, Faizan Ahmad, Aidan Boyd, et al.
This report documents the preparedness assessment of Code World Model (CWM), a model for code generation and reasoning about code from Meta. We conducted pre-release testing across domains identified in our Frontier AI Framework as potentially presenting catastrophic risks, and also evaluated the mo...

item.title: Hierarchical Abstract Tree for Cross-Document Retrieval-Augmented Generation
item.description:
By Ziwen Zhao, Menglin Yang
Retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) enhances large language models with external knowledge, and tree-based RAG organizes documents into hierarchical indexes to support queries at multiple granularities. However, existing Tree-RAG methods designed for single-document retrieval face critical challeng...

item.title: Persistent Visual Memory: Sustaining Perception for Deep Generation in LVLMs
item.description:
By Siyuan Huang, Xiaoye Qu, Yafu Li, Tong Zhu, Zefeng He, et al.
While autoregressive Large Vision-Language Models (LVLMs) demonstrate remarkable proficiency in multimodal tasks, they face a "Visual Signal Dilution" phenomenon, where the accumulation of textual history expands the attention partition function, causing visual attention to decay inversely with gene...

item.title: From Context to Skills: Can Language Models Learn from Context Skillfully?
item.description:
By Shuzheng Si, Haozhe Zhao, Yu Lei, Qingyi Wang, Dingwei Chen, et al.
Many real-world tasks require language models (LMs) to reason over complex contexts that exceed their parametric knowledge. This calls for context learning, where LMs directly learn relevant knowledge from the given context. An intuitive solution is inference-time skill augmentation: extracting the ...

item.title: Motion-Aware Caching for Efficient Autoregressive Video Generation
item.description:
By Jing Xu, Yuexiao Ma, Songwei Liu, Xuzhe Zheng, Shiwei Liu, et al.
Autoregressive video generation paradigms offer theoretical promise for long video synthesis, yet their practical deployment is hindered by the computational burden of sequential iterative denoising. While cache reuse strategies can accelerate generation by skipping redundant denoising steps, existi...

item.title: PhysicianBench: Evaluating LLM Agents in Real-World EHR Environments
item.description:
By Ruoqi Liu, Imran Q. Mohiuddin, Austin J. Schoeffler, Kavita Renduchintala, Ashwin Nayak, et al.
We introduce PhysicianBench, a benchmark for evaluating LLM agents on physician tasks grounded in real clinical setting within electronic health record (EHR) environments. Existing medical agent benchmarks primarily focus on static knowledge recall, single-step atomic actions, or action intent witho...

item.title: T^2PO: Uncertainty-Guided Exploration Control for Stable Multi-Turn Agentic Reinforcement Learning
item.description:
By Haixin Wang, Hejie Cui, Chenwei Zhang, Xin Liu, Shuowei Jin, et al.
Recent progress in multi-turn reinforcement learning (RL) has significantly improved reasoning LLMs' performances on complex interactive tasks. Despite advances in stabilization techniques such as fine-grained credit assignment and trajectory filtering, instability remains pervasive and often leads ...

item.title: AcademiClaw: When Students Set Challenges for AI Agents
item.description:
By Junjie Yu, Pengrui Lu, Weiye Si, Hongliang Lu, Jiabao Wu, et al.
Benchmarks within the OpenClaw ecosystem have thus far evaluated exclusively assistant-level tasks, leaving the academic-level capabilities of OpenClaw largely unexamined. We introduce AcademiClaw, a bilingual benchmark of 80 complex, long-horizon tasks sourced directly from university students' rea...

item.title: Perceptual Flow Network for Visually Grounded Reasoning
item.description:
By Yangfu Li, Yuning Gong, Hongjian Zhan, Teng Li, Yuanhuiyi Lyu, et al.
Despite the success of Large-Vision Language Models (LVLMs), general optimization objectives (e.g., standard MLE) fail to constrain visual trajectories, leading to language bias and hallucination. To mitigate this, current methods introduce geometric priors from visual experts as additional supervis...

item.title: Generative Modeling with Orbit-Space Particle Flow Matching
item.description:
By Sinan Wang, Jinjin He, Shenyifan Lu, Ruicheng Wang, Greg Turk, et al.
We present Orbit-Space Geometric Probability Paths (OGPP), a particle-native flow-matching framework for generative modeling of particle systems. OGPP is motivated by two insights: (i) particles are defined up to permutation symmetries, so anonymous indexing inflates per-index target variance and yi...

item.title: MolmoAct2: Action Reasoning Models for Real-world Deployment
item.description:
By Haoquan Fang, Jiafei Duan, Donovan Clay, Sam Wang, Shuo Liu, et al.
Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models aim to provide a single generalist controller for robots, but today's systems fall short on the criteria that matter for real-world deployment. Frontier models are closed, open-weight alternatives are tied to expensive hardware, reasoning-augmented policies pay pr...
